ud2.9:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī at the Eastern Monastery, the palace of Migāra’s mother.
ud2.9:1.3: And on that occasion, Visākhā, Migāra’s mother,
ud2.9:1.4: had some dealings with King Pasenadi Kosala that he did not settle as she had wished.
ud2.9:2.1: So in the middle of the day she went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, having bowed down to him, sat to one side.
ud2.9:2.2: As she was sitting there the Blessed One said to her,
ud2.9:2.3: “Well now, Visākhā, where are you coming from in the middle of the day?”
ud2.9:2.4: “Just now, lord, I had some dealings with King Pasenadi Kosala that he did not settle as I had wished.”
ud2.9:3.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud2.9:4.1: All subjection to others is painful.
ud2.9:4.3: What is held in common brings suffering,
ud2.9:4.4: for duties are hard to overcome.
